org.exist.schema
Class GenericSchemaService

java.lang.Object
  org.exist.schema.GenericSchemaService
All Implemented Interfaces:
Configurable, SchemaAccess, SchemaService, Service
Direct Known Subclasses:
LocalSchemaService, RemoteSchemaService
public abstract class GenericSchemaService
extends java.lang.Object
implements SchemaService

Author:
seb

                         throws XMLDBException
Description copied from interface: Service
Returns the name associated with the Service instance.

the name of the object.

public java.lang.String getProperty(java.lang.String arg0)
                             throws XMLDBException
Description copied from interface: Configurable
Returns the value of the property identified by name.

Specified by:
getProperty in interface Configurable
Parameters:
arg0 - the name of the property to retrieve.
Returns:
the property value or null if no property exists.

XMLDBException - with expected error codes.
ErrorCodes.VENDOR_ERROR for any vendor specific errors that occur.

                        java.lang.String arg1)
                 throws XMLDBException

Sets the property name to have the value provided in value.

Specified by:
setProperty in interface Configurable
Parameters:
arg0 - the name of the property to set.
arg1 - the value to set for the property.
Throws:
XMLDBException - with expected error codes.
ErrorCodes.VENDOR_ERROR for any vendor specific errors that occur.

putSchema

public void putSchema(java.lang.String schemaContents)
               throws XMLDBException
Add a schema to the schema store. The schema must have a target namespace because it can otherwise not be indexed.

Specified by:
putSchema in interface SchemaService
Throws:
XMLDBException

getSchema

public XMLResource getSchema(java.lang.String targetNamespace)
                      throws XMLDBException
Retrieves the schema as an XML resources.

Specified by:
getSchema in interface SchemaService
Returns:
the schema with targetNamespace or null if that schema is not known.
Throws:
XMLDBException

validateContents

public boolean validateContents(java.lang.String contents)
                         throws XMLDBException
Validates the passed contents. Schemas are automatically obtained from the schema store. You can add transient ("temporary") schemas with the registerTransientSchema method.

Specified by:
validateContents in interface SchemaService
Throws:
XMLDBException - if a database error occurs or the contents contains validation errors (these are wrapped in XMLDBExceptions).

validateResource

public boolean validateResource(java.lang.String id)
                         throws XMLDBException
Validates a resource given its id. Uses validateContents.

Specified by:
validateResource in interface SchemaService
Throws:
XMLDBException

getAttribute in interface SchemaAccess
Returns:
the attribute by name qname or null if no such attribute is known.

XMLDBException

                                                    throws XMLDBException

                                             throws XMLDBException

Is a schema defining this namespace known

registerTransientSchema

public void registerTransientSchema(java.lang.String schema)
                             throws XMLDBException
Description copied from interface: SchemaService
Add a schema on-the-fly. This schema will not be made persistent. This is usefull to validate documents where one knows that a schema is not in the schema store.

Specified by:
registerTransientSchema in interface SchemaService
Parameters:
schema -
Throws:
XMLDBException

getTransientSchemas

public java.util.ArrayList getTransientSchemas()
Returns:

                  throws XMLDBException

Delete and recreate the index file based on the schema resources stored in the /db/system/schema collection

Specified by:
rebuildIndex in interface SchemaService
Throws:
XMLDBException
